perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to anyone they deem necessary, regardless of whether they are employees, agents, consultants or contractors. No written agreements are necessary for such disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts disclosure to specific individuals with written agreements. The changed text allows disclosure to anyone without any agreements, directly contradicting the original limitation. This creates uncertainty on who the recipient can share the confidential information with.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law, and in addition may recover all reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in sufficient time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for written agreements for those receiving confidential information, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which outlines remedies for breaches of the agreement, including scenarios where confidential information is sought by third parties. The original text enforces Recipient's liability through written agreements with downstream parties who have access to the confidential information. The modified text makes it difficult if not impossible to enforce provisions against downstream parties, creating a direct conflict and undermining the Disclosing Party's rights and remedies in case of a breach related to the confidentiality of the information.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient may, at their discretion, return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof. If the Recipient doesn't want to return it, they are not obligated to.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The changed text makes it optional ('may, at their discretion'), contradicting the original mandatory obligation. This creates uncertainty about the recipient's obligations regarding the return of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The changed text makes it optional ('may, at their discretion'), directly conflicting with the original mandatory obligation and undermining the Disclosing Party's rights over its Confidential Information as outlined in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confident ial Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party which had the right to and transmits it to the Rec ipient without any obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, regardless of whether the Recipient caused it to be public; or (b) at any time received from any third party, even if that third party breached a duty of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text excludes information that becomes public through no fault of the Recipient or is rightfully received from a third party. The modified version broadens this to include information that becomes public *due to* the Recipient's actions and information received even when the third party breached their duty. This contradicts the implicit obligation to protect confidential information and changes the scope of what's considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient ag rees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but i n no event with less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purp ose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly broadens the exceptions to the confidentiality obligations. The original text excludes information that becomes public through no fault of the Recipient or is rightfully received. The changed text includes information becoming public due to the Recipient's actions and information received even when the third party breached their duty. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which requires the Recipient to keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information confidential and protect it with a reasonable degree of care. The changed text would allow the Recipient to disclose information and then claim it is no longer confidential because they caused it to enter the public domain, negating the confidentiality obligation and thus a strong in-text contradiction according to Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law, and in addition may recover all reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient *intends* to breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party may, at its sole discretion, be entitled to seek injunctive relief only. No monetary damages may be sought, regardless of the severity of the breach, only if the recipient *intends* to breach the contract.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for all legal and equitable remedies, including monetary damages, for a breach or imminent breach. The changed text limits the Disclosing Party to injunctive relief *only* and only if the Recipient *intends* to breach the contract, contradicting the original broad remedies available for any breach. This creates uncertainty about the Disclosing Party's recourse in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be gover ned in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties. This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. Disclosing Party: Company: _______________________ Name: __________________________ Role: ___________________________ Phone Number: __________________ Signature: ___________________________ ____ The Recipient : Company: _____ Pitch Deck Fire ______ Name: _______ Stacie Shaw __________ Role: _________ Founder ___________ Phone Number: __ _214-770-9216 ____ Signature: _______________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for 'all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law,' including monetary damages, for a breach or imminent breach. The changed text limits the Disclosing Party to 'injunctive relief only' and only if the Recipient 'intends' to breach the contract. The 'Contradicted Text' section does not create the contradiction. The contradiction stems from the original text allowing all remedies, and the change limiting remedies, causing a clear and direct conflict within the contract regarding available remedies for a breach.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended orally by any representative of either party. Written amendments are encouraged but not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires written amendments signed by authorized representatives. The change allows for oral amendments by *any* representative, directly contradicting the original requirement for written and authorized amendments. The sentence in this change creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended orally by any representative of either party. Written amendments are encouraged but not required. This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. Disclosing Party: Company: _______________________ Name: __________________________ Role: ___________________________ Phone Number: __________________ Signature: ___________________________ ____ The Recipient : Company: _____ Pitch Deck Fire ______ Name: _______ Stacie Shaw __________ Role: _________ Founder ___________ Phone Number: __ _214-770-9216 ____ Signature: _______________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates amendments to be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. The changed text permits oral amendments by *any* representative, which directly contradicts the initial requirement, thereby creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws. The contradicted text repeats the allowance of oral amendments by any representative, highlighting the contradiction.
